briefdescription.para: Callback function for FAT file system timestamp generation.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used by the SdFat library to obtain current date and time for file system operations. It retrieves the time from the RTC and converts it to the FAT file system format using the appropriate macros.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to store the encoded FAT date (year, month, day)
Pointer to store the encoded FAT time (hour, minute, second)

simplesect.para: This function is registered as a callback with SdFile::dateTimeCallback()
FAT_DATE, FAT_TIME macros for encoding format details

briefdescription.para: Initializes all core system components and peripherals.

detaileddescription.para: This function performs comprehensive system initialization including:

**Network Setup:**
Establishes WiFi connection with timeout handling
Configures MQTT client with unique sensor-based ID
Attempts initial MQTT broker connection

**Hardware Initialization:**
Initializes DS3231 real-time clock module
Adjusts RTC time if power was lost (uses compilation timestamp)
Sets up SD card with SPI communication
Initializes ADT7410 temperature sensor

**Data Recovery:**
Registers FAT file system timestamp callback
Attempts to send any pending/recovered data from previous sessions

simplesect.para: This function will halt program execution (infinite loop) if any critical component fails to initialize (RTC, SD card, or temperature sensor)
The function uses compile-time constants for timeouts and configuration

briefdescription.para: Main operational loop for continuous sensor monitoring and data transmission.

detaileddescription.para: This function implements the core operational logic of the temperature monitoring system with robust connectivity handling and data persistence features.

1. **Time Management:**
Reads current time from RTC
Tracks minute changes to prevent duplicate measurements

2. **WiFi Connection Management:**
Monitors WiFi status with intelligent reconnection timing
Falls back to CSV logging during WiFi outages
Implements reconnection throttling to prevent excessive attempts

3. **MQTT Connection Management:**
Verifies MQTT broker connectivity
Handles automatic reconnection with status reporting
Falls back to CSV logging during MQTT outages

4. **Data Recovery Processing:**
Sends pending CSV data after successful reconnection
Ensures recovery data is transmitted only once per reconnection cycle

5. **Normal Operation:**
Performs temperature measurements once per minute
Transmits data via MQTT to configured topic
Maintains MQTT client polling for incoming messages

**Error Handling:**
Network failures trigger CSV fallback storage
Connection attempts are rate-limited to prevent resource exhaustion
All measurement data is preserved during connectivity issues

simplesect.para: The function maintains a fixed loop delay for consistent timing
